growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: bacdive-microa.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: corkrey.
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: optimized artificial-seawater (ASW) magnetotactic medium (Silva et al. 2013, ref 21)

up.method: HPLC (Aminex HPX-87H) monitoring; consumed throughout growth, at 120 h remaining = 62% of inoculation concentration; specific rate (mmol/gDW/h) not derivable (no gDW biomass reported)
ferrozine colorimetric kit; largest consumption 48-72 h; specific rate not quantified
potentiometric (Unisense electrode); ~80% consumed during exponential phase, largest consumption 48-72 h
ninhydrin colorimetric; ~70% remaining at 96 h; consumption tracks growth not magnetite

sec.method: TEM magnetosome count x volume; max productivity 39.7 mg/L/day at 72 h

growth_records.cite: Correa et al. Continuous Production of Biogenic Magnetite Nanoparticles by the Marine Bacterium Magnetovibrio blakemorei Strain MV-1T with a Nitrous Oxide Injection Strategy. Marine Drugs (2022). DOI:10.3390/md20110724. [PMC9692579]
growth_records.snippet: the highest specific growth rate observed between 24 h and 48 h (mu max = 0.05 h-1 ...). Magnetite production, whose maximum value reached 72 h (p = 32.5 mg/L ...). Maximum magnetite productivity was reached at 72 h (p = 1.65 mg/L/h or 39.7
growth_records.notes: FED-BATCH. mu_max=0.05 h-1 (calculated from ln X slope 24-48 h via Eq. mu=(lnX2-lnX1)/(t2-t1)); td=ln2/0.05=13.86 h. Cell density calibration OD600 1.0 = 1.09e10 cells/mL. Substrate (succinate, Fe2+, N2O, amino-N) consumption measured but only as percent-remaining, not specific gDW rates, so rate fields null (no biomass gDW to compute qS). Magnetite (intracellular biomineral product) titer 32.5 mg/L, productivity 39.7 mg/L/day at 72 h; reported volumetrically.

growth_records.medium: optimized artificial-seawater (ASW) magnetotactic medium (Silva et al. 2013, ref 21)

up.method: HPLC monitoring at steady state; kept approximately constant; specific rate not quantified

sec.method: TEM count x volume; 27.1 mg/L at 120 h, 26.1 mg/L at 168 h; productivity 22.7 mg/L/day

growth_records.cite: Correa et al. Continuous Production of Biogenic Magnetite Nanoparticles by the Marine Bacterium Magnetovibrio blakemorei Strain MV-1T with a Nitrous Oxide Injection Strategy. Marine Drugs (2022). DOI:10.3390/md20110724. [PMC9692579]
growth_records.snippet: We only employed one dilution rate (D = 0.035 h-1), which was equivalent to 70% of the growth rate in the exponential phase (24-72 h) ... reaching 26.1 mg/L at 168 h. The total magnetite produced here was 104.2 mg
growth_records.notes: CHEMOSTAT continuous culture. D=0.035 h-1 (=70% of fed-batch mu_max 0.05 h-1). At steady state mu=D=0.035 h-1; td=ln2/0.035=19.8 h. Magnetite 27.1 mg/L (120 h), 26.1 mg/L (168 h), productivity 22.7 mg/L/day, total 104.2 mg. Substrate consumption monitored but not gDW-normalized. Only one D and one residence time tested.

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 23250 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/23250
